title: [InstCombine]  Widen Sel width after Cmp to generate Max/Min intrinsics.

tests.source_program:
define zeroext i8 @zext_through_cast_unsigned_min(i8 %a, i32 %min) {
  %a32 = zext i8 %a to i32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %a32, %min
  %min8 = trunc i32 %min to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 %a, i8 %min8
  ret i8 %sel
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define zeroext i8 @zext_through_cast_unsigned_min(i8 %a, i32 %min) {
  %a32 = zext i8 %a to i32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %a32, %min
  %widesel = select i1 %cmp, i32 %a32, i32 %min
  %tr = trunc i32 %widesel to i8
  ret i8 %tr
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions sext/zext compatibility but all existing tests use sext only. This uses zext with unsigned comparison (ult) which is not explicitly covered in the test examples.

alive2.src:
define zeroext i8 @zext_through_cast_unsigned_min(i8 %a, i32 %min) {
  %a32 = zext i8 %a to i32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %a32, %min
  %min8 = trunc i32 %min to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 %a, i8 %min8
  ret i8 %sel
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define zeroext i8 @zext_through_cast_unsigned_min(i8 %a, i32 %min) {
  %a32 = zext i8 %a to i32
  %sel1 = call i32 @llvm.umin.i32(i32 %min, i32 %a32)
  %sel = trunc nuw i32 %sel1 to i8
  ret i8 %sel
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.umin.i32(i32, i32) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @zext_through_cast_unsigned_min(i8 %a, i32 %min) zeroext {
#0:
  %a32 = zext i8 %a to i32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %a32, %min
  %min8 = trunc i32 %min to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 %a, i8 %min8
  ret i8 %sel
}
=>
define i8 @zext_through_cast_unsigned_min(i8 %a, i32 %min) zeroext {
#0:
  %a32 = zext i8 %a to i32
  %sel1 = umin i32 %min, %a32
  %sel = trunc nuw i32 %sel1 to i8
  ret i8 %sel
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
define i16 @i64_to_i16_trunc_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %max) {
  %a64 = sext i16 %a to i64
  %cmp = icmp sgt i64 %max, %a64
  %max16 = trunc i64 %max to i16
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i16 %max16, i16 %a
  ret i16 %sel
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i16 @i64_to_i16_trunc_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %max) {
  %a64 = sext i16 %a to i64
  %cmp = icmp sgt i64 %max, %a64
  %widesel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %max, i64 %a64
  %tr = trunc i64 %widesel to i16
  ret i16 %tr
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary covers width changes but existing tests only show i32->i8, i32->i16, i64->i32. This i64->i16 direct truncation pattern with max comparison is a width combination not in the test examples.

alive2.src:
define i16 @i64_to_i16_trunc_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %max) {
  %a64 = sext i16 %a to i64
  %cmp = icmp sgt i64 %max, %a64
  %max16 = trunc i64 %max to i16
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i16 %max16, i16 %a
  ret i16 %sel
}

source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i16 @i64_to_i16_trunc_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %max) {
  %a64 = sext i16 %a to i64
  %sel1 = call i64 @llvm.smax.i64(i64 %max, i64 %a64)
  %sel = trunc i64 %sel1 to i16
  ret i16 %sel
}

tests.source_program:
define zeroext i16 @zext_ult_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %bound) {
  %a64 = zext i16 %a to i64
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %a64, %bound
  %bound16 = trunc i64 %bound to i16
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i16 %a, i16 %bound16
  ret i16 %sel
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define zeroext i16 @zext_ult_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %bound) {
  %a64 = zext i16 %a to i64
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %a64, %bound
  %widesel = select i1 %cmp, i64 %a64, i64 %bound
  %tr = trunc i64 %widesel to i16
  ret i16 %tr
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes sext/signed comparisons (slt/sgt), but this uses zext with unsigned comparison (ult). The golden patch handles ZExtOrSExt generically, but the summary text focuses on signed extension patterns.

alive2.src:
define zeroext i16 @zext_ult_pattern(i16 %a, i64 %bound) {
  %a64 = zext i16 %a to i64
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %a64, %bound
  %bound16 = trunc i64 %bound to i16
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i16 %a, i16 %bound16
  ret i16 %sel
}
